title: Authenticated Encryption with AES-GCM · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds versioned AES-GCM record envelope, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned AES-GCM record envelope while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.
Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.
Bind protocol version, tenant, object identity, algorithm suite, direction, and other non-secret context as canonical associated data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Aead Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 4935 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Aead Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use aead seal and open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Aead Contract, keep Crypto Go Aead Contract connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-gcm-failure-uniformity-1ob42e" that uses case 6686 to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.

At Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 7268 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Collapse attacker-visible authentication failures while retaining safe internal classification and metrics.

For Cryptography and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Detailed tag, key, nonce, and padding errors help clients recover securely.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 8887 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Collapse attacker-visible authentication failures while retaining safe internal classification and metrics.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
